notes: Historical figure mentioned by Plutarch as a guide to Crassus, described as faithless.

docs.title: Life of Crassus
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … be known to Andromachus, the most faithless of men, nay he was so infatuated as to choose him for his guide. The Parthians then, to be sure, had punctual intelligence of all that passed; but it being contrary to their usage, and also difficult for them to fight by night, and Crassus having
